books.title: Joe and the Wreck of the Tribune
books.author: Jacqueline Halsey
books.rationale: At the turn of the nineteenth century, a thirteen-year old boy risked his life to save drowning sailors from the wreck of the H.M.S. Tribune, off the coast of Nova Scotia. This historical fiction novel imagines a backstory for the boy only known to history as "Joe Cracker," and it paints a detailed picture of what life was like in a small fishing village around 1800.

books.title: The Story of Canada
books.author: Janet Lunn, Christopher Moore
books.rationale: Canadian history is told through rich narrative, recreations of daily life, folk tales, and fascinating facts.
books.scopeText:
Term 1 (Partial Term)
Term 2 (Partial Term)
Term 3 (Partial Term)
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 5-6
Canadian History: Grades 7-8

books.title: The Loxleys and Confederation
books.author: Mark Zuehlke
books.rationale: The story of Canada's Confederation of 1867 told in a graphic novel format. Students meet key historical figures whose debates and vision shaped the course of Canada's history. The story also introduces other significant events during the period, and includes a written afterward that serves as a historical overview.

books.title: Tecumseh
books.author: James Laxer
books.rationale: This book personalizes the events of the War of 1812 by presenting it through the life and experiences of Tecumseh, a Shawnee chief and close friend and ally of Major General Isaac Brock. Written by a historian of nineteenth-century Canada and biographer of Tecumseh, this book introduces readers to the important role played by Indigenous groups in the wars that shaped Canada.
